UT contempt — $1,000 fine (higher than most) and 30 days jail. Broad trigger.
UT discovery sanctions escalate to contempt level. Strongest enforcement available.
UT dissipation remedy — greater share. Standard equitable distribution approach.
UT remedial sanctions — standard coercive model with present ability requirement.
UT dual qualifier — substantial AND not contemplated. Similar to SC. Higher bar for modification.
UT broad fee authority — includes expert witness fees. Leveling the playing field framing.
UT unique — mandatory 10 hours compensatory service. Plus workshops. Criminal option via §76-5-303.

entries.word: without merit and not in good faith
entries.notes: UT mandatory fees for denied meritless modification — shall order. Dual test: without merit AND bad faith.
UT mandatory court-approved form. Standard financial disclosure. All family cases.
UT only exception is absconding debtors — narrower than most state fraud exceptions.
OK contempt — $500 fine and/or 6 months for failure to comply with ANY child-related order. Broad trigger.
OK custody enforcement through contempt. Affirmative defense for emergency/protective custody is notable.
OK surcharge — unique remedy charging waste against offending spouse share. Filing date valuation available.
OK separates discovery sanctions from frivolous filing sanctions — dedicated discovery code framework.
Standard modification trigger — continuing jurisdiction. OK uses standard single-qualifier approach.
OK automatic injunction upon filing — violation is contempt. Prevents asset disposal without separate motion.
OK unique — Supreme Court guidelines for purge fees. Three statutory factors. Systematic approach to purge.
OK ties fees to enforcement/modification actions. Mandatory for DV victims — paid by abuser before and after final order.
OK frivolous standard — bad faith or no rational argument. Deterrence-limited. Does not apply to discovery.
Narrow exception — only fines/penalties for law violation. Support excepted by case law. Defensive only.
